paper_title: Field Sampling of Sediments and Foraminifera

methods.description: Process for collecting sediment and water samples from the Santa Barbara Basin using various coring and sampling techniques to study geochemical properties and foraminiferal content.

steps.description: Samples were obtained from 3 to 5 May 2018 using the R/V Robert Gordon Sproul.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples using R/V Robert Gordon Sproul

steps.description: A CTDO 2 cast was typically the first sampling event at each site.
steps.searchable_summary: perform CTDO 2 cast at each site

steps.description: Bottom waters were collected from Niskin bottles deployed on the CTDO 2 rosette.
steps.searchable_summary: collect bottom waters using Niskin bottles

steps.description: An Ocean Instruments MC800 multicorer obtained sediments for geochemical sampling at fine scale.
steps.searchable_summary: use MC800 multicorer to collect sediment samples

steps.description: As soon as the multicorer was on deck, the multicores were taken into the environmental room, set near bottom-water temperature (7°C), and processed as required.
steps.searchable_summary: process multicores in environmental room at 7°C

steps.description: A Soutar box corer obtained copious amounts of foraminiferal-bearing surface sediments.
steps.searchable_summary: use Soutar box corer to collect surface sediments

steps.description: Immediately after recovering the boxcorer, its lid was opened, and large-bore plastic tubing was used to siphon off most overlying water.
steps.searchable_summary: siphon off overlying water from boxcorer

steps.description: A custom-made three-sided spatula was used to collect the surface ~1 to 2 cm of sediment, which was placed into a clean zipper-lock plastic bag.
steps.searchable_summary: collect surface sediment with spatula into plastic bag

steps.description: Sediment-laden bags were immediately taken into the darkened environmental van, where the sediment slurries were dispensed into HDPE plastic bottles to a depth of approximately 2 cm and then filled with bottom water so no overlying air was entrapped.
steps.searchable_summary: dispense sediment slurries into HDPE bottles with bottom water

steps.description: Surface sediment from each boxcore was divided among approximately 20 bottles; these were maintained in the environmental room in the dark until transport to WHOI on blue ice in coolers.
steps.searchable_summary: divide surface sediment into bottles and store in environmental room

steps.description: At WHOI, samples were kept in the dark in coolers inside an environmental room set at 7°C until use.
steps.searchable_summary: store samples in WHOI environmental room at 7°C

methods.description: Collection of sediments for specific species of foraminifera for experimental treatments.

steps.description: For each species, sediments from one boxcore were used for each experimental treatment.
steps.searchable_summary: use boxcore sediments for species-specific experiments

steps.description: For N. stella, sediments were collected on 5 May from a water depth of 567 m (34° 19.293 N, 120° 03.472 W).
steps.searchable_summary: collect N. stella sediments from specified location

steps.description: B. argentea were collected on 3 May from a water depth of 428.5 m (34° 18.088 N, 119° 54.557 W).
steps.searchable_summary: collect B. argentea sediments from specified location

steps.description: Some foraminiferal cells from both species were picked from sieved sediment on shipboard immediately after sediment collections.
steps.searchable_summary: pick foraminiferal cells from sieved sediment
